summary: W856 keeps the target nonterminal under the immutable original four-task contract while deriving the W720 forgetful homology-preservation endpoint subfield from the existing W519 mapped-cokernel closedness branch via W503/W694. It introduces a weakened endpoint-facts route-data provider whose endpoint input now contains only closed-embedding, open-map, and ExactAt-to-epi facts; W856 reconstructs the full W720 provider and feeds the W853 homology-free selected-cochain route. Product success remains false because the remaining W856 route data fields, the final original-four-task witness, and the completion review packet are still absent.
known_gaps: Task 4 is not contract-complete under the original four-task contract: W856 removes the separate W720 forgetful homology-preservation endpoint subfield, but the route still needs concrete boundary, relation, target-surjective/compact, matched closedness branch, normalized target, source triangulation, three endpoint topology facts, and localized-right-adjoint data.
The final Dbounded MetrizableLCA original four-task completion witness is still missing; ProductSuccessDeclarations and OriginalFourTaskCompletionDeclarations only record nonterminal route evidence.
The review packet still does not expose the immutable-contract negative fixtures, external SHA256 sidecar, and clean extraction audit required for product success.
remaining_work: Construct concrete values for the W856 endpoint-facts homology-free route-data provider, starting with one of the eight remaining fields or one of the three endpoint topology facts.
Expose a no-argument or fully instance-resolved original four-task completion witness after the route-data provider and stable Dbounded data are real.
Refresh the review packet fixtures, manifest, clean extraction audit, and terminal outcome only after the original witness exists.
latest_verified_increment: W856 endpoint topology from closedness branch
